title: Ruby and gold flared lehenga for an Anand Karaj
concept_summary: A luminous Anand Karaj concept built around a full-flared ruby lehenga with a fitted, fully covered choli and a double dupatta with one layer drawn over the head. Gold zardozi catches the light while the mood stays reverent and restrained for the Sikh marriage ceremony.

garment_breakdown.overall_form: A fitted, high-necked choli paired with a voluminous circular lehenga skirt and a double dupatta.
garment_breakdown.silhouette: A cleanly fitted upper body opening into a wide, evenly gathered floor-length flare.
garment_breakdown.drape_or_layering: One dupatta is drawn over the head while the second rests along the body.
garment_breakdown.key_proportions: A close bodice, covered waist and a full skirt keep the proportions balanced and formal.

colour_story.palette_summary: A ruby base lifted by warm gold, kept rich rather than harsh.
colour_story.placement: Ruby leads across the skirt and choli, with gold as the accent through the embroidery and border.
colour_story.rationale: Ruby reads celebratory for an Anand Karaj, while gold adds warmth without overwhelming the look.

fabrics_and_texture.finish_and_movement: A smooth, lustrous-faced weave that catches light cleanly and holds the skirt's flare.
A crisp, translucent layer that floats lightly over the head and shoulders.

embellishment_plan.density: A balanced amount of work concentrated at borders and the bodice rather than all over.
embellishment_plan.restraint_notes: Open ruby ground is left between motifs so the metalwork stays elegant.

coverage_and_drape.sleeves: Full-length fitted sleeves reaching the wrists as requested.
coverage_and_drape.neckline: A high, fully closed neckline covering the collarbone and upper chest.
coverage_and_drape.back_and_midriff: A fully covered midriff with no bare skin at the waist.
coverage_and_drape.head_covering: One dupatta layer is worn over the head throughout, honouring the stated preference.
coverage_and_drape.dupatta_or_saree_drape: A double dupatta, one layer over the head and one down the body.

cultural_context.regional_direction: A broad Punjabi bridal influence guides the restraint and metalwork emphasis.
interpretation_notes: Treated as one broad regional direction, not a fixed rule for any community.
safeguards: The Anand Karaj is treated as the Sikh marriage ceremony and never conflated with other rites.

styling_notes: Keep jewellery warm-toned to echo the gold work.
A covered hairstyle suits the head drape.
construction_caveats: This is a concept visualisation, not a sewing pattern, and includes no measurements.
It does not guarantee that the garment can be constructed exactly as shown.
image_alt_text: A model in a ruby flared lehenga with gold embroidery, a high neckline and a double dupatta drawn over the head for an Anand Karaj.
